seoTitle: How to Set Up SEO Features in Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to use Wix's SEO Setup Checklist, meta tags, and Google Search Console integration to boost search visibility.
intro: Wix includes a built-in SEO toolkit that guides you through the essentials, from meta titles to sitemap submission, without needing separate plugins. This guide covers running the SEO Setup Checklist, customizing individual page metadata, and connecting your site to Google Search Console for indexing.

steps.heading: Open the Marketing & SEO panel
steps.body: From your Wix dashboard, access the central Marketing & SEO control panel.

steps.heading: Run the SEO Setup Checklist
steps.body: Launch the checklist and input your business name, location, and core target keywords.

steps.heading: Edit individual page SEO settings
steps.body: In the Pages and Menu directory, customize meta titles, descriptions, and URL slugs for each page.

steps.heading: Review structured data and canonical tags
steps.body: Check the advanced SEO tabs for schema markup and canonical tag settings.

steps.heading: Add alt text to images
steps.body: Apply descriptive, keyword-relevant alt text across your image and media elements.

steps.heading: Connect Google Search Console and submit your sitemap
steps.body: Authorize the connection from your dashboard and submit your XML sitemap for indexing.

faq.question: Do I need a third-party SEO plugin for Wix?
faq.answer: No, Wix's built-in SEO Setup Checklist and page-level settings cover most of the essentials without needing extra plugins.

faq.question: How do I connect my Wix site to Google Search Console?
faq.answer: Wix provides a direct authorization flow from your dashboard that syncs your sitemap automatically once connected.

faq.question: What should I put in a page's meta description?
faq.answer: A concise summary of that specific page's content that includes relevant keywords naturally, written to encourage clicks from search results.

faq.question: Does adding alt text to images actually help SEO?
faq.answer: Yes, descriptive alt text helps search engines understand image content and can contribute to overall page relevance.
